title: Pong Dam Lake Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Pong Dam Lake Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Himachal Pradesh

heroImage.alt: Pong Dam Lake Wildlife Sanctuary, Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: Pong Dam Wild Life Sanctuary is a sanctuary in Himachal Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Pong Dam Wild Life Sanctuary is a sanctuary in Himachal Pradesh, India. It covers 673 square kilometers. It was established in November 2002, under the Wildlife Protection Act of 1972. The sanctuary protects Maharana Pratap Sagar lake & wetland, which gained Ramsar Convention for International importance in 2002.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Maharana Pratap Sagar
topPlaces.description: Maharana Pratap Sagar, also known as Pong Reservoir or Pong Dam Lake, is a large reservoir in Fatehpur, Jawali and Dehra tehsil of Kangra district of the state of Himachal Pradesh in India. It was created in 1975, by building the highest earthfill dam in India on the Beas River in the wetland zone of the Siwalik Hills.

topPlaces.name: Nagrota Surian
topPlaces.description: Nagrota Surian is a town in Nagrota Surian Mandal, Kangra District, in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. Nagrota Surian is 45.2 km from the district headquarters Dharamshala.

topPlaces.name: Sakri, Kangra
topPlaces.description: Sakri is a village located in Fatehpur Tehsil of Kangra district in Himachal Pradesh, India. Rehan is the nearest town to this village.

topPlaces.name: Dhami State
topPlaces.description: Dhami was a Princely State situated 26 kilometres (16 mi) west of Shimla, India. Its capital was Halog and the state formed a part of the region known as the Punjab Hill States Agency during the British Raj period.

topPlaces.name: Haripur Guler
topPlaces.description: Haripur is a township in Kangra district in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. Haripur and Guler are twin townships carrying the heritage of Guler Riyasat.

topPlaces.name: Guler chieftaincy
topPlaces.description: Guler was a minor chieftaincy in the Lower Himalayas. Its capital was the town of Haripur Guler, in modern-day Himachal Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Masrur Temples
topPlaces.description: The Masrur Temples, also referred to as Masroor Temples or Rock-cut Temples at Masrur, is an early 8th-century complex of rock-cut Hindu temples in the Kangra Valley of Beas River in Himachal Pradesh, India. The temples face northeast, towards the Dhauladhar range of the Himalayas.

topPlaces.name: Bathu Temples
topPlaces.description: Bathu temples (बाथू मंदिर), known locally as Bathu ki ladi (बाथू की लड़ी), is a cluster of temples in the Kangra district of Indian state of Himachal Pradesh, with the main temple dedicated to goddess Parvati and Lord Shiva. These temples were submerged in Maharana Pratap Sagar, a reservoir created by Pong dam in the early 1970s.

items.note: Maharana Pratap Sagar, also known as Pong Reservoir or Pong Dam Lake, is a large reservoir in Fatehpur, Jawali and Dehra tehsil of Kangra district of the state of Himachal Pradesh in India. It was created in 1975, by building the highest earthfill dam in India on the Beas River in the wetland zone of the Siwalik Hills.
Nagrota Surian is a town in Nagrota Surian Mandal, Kangra District, in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. Nagrota Surian is 45.2 km from the district headquarters Dharamshala.
Sakri is a village located in Fatehpur Tehsil of Kangra district in Himachal Pradesh, India. Rehan is the nearest town to this village.

Dhami was a Princely State situated 26 kilometres (16 mi) west of Shimla, India. Its capital was Halog and the state formed a part of the region known as the Punjab Hill States Agency during the British Raj period.
Haripur is a township in Kangra district in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. Haripur and Guler are twin townships carrying the heritage of Guler Riyasat.
Guler was a minor chieftaincy in the Lower Himalayas. Its capital was the town of Haripur Guler, in modern-day Himachal Pradesh.

The Masrur Temples, also referred to as Masroor Temples or Rock-cut Temples at Masrur, is an early 8th-century complex of rock-cut Hindu temples in the Kangra Valley of Beas River in Himachal Pradesh, India. The temples face northeast, towards the Dhauladhar range of the Himalayas.

Bathu temples (बाथू मंदिर), known locally as Bathu ki ladi (बाथू की लड़ी), is a cluster of temples in the Kangra district of Indian state of Himachal Pradesh, with the main temple dedicated to goddess Parvati and Lord Shiva. These temples were submerged in Maharana Pratap Sagar, a reservoir created by Pong dam in the early 1970s.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pong Dam Lake Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pong Dam Lake Wildlife Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Pong Dam Lake Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kangra Airport (Gaggal), Dharamshala (24 km) and the nearest railway station is Amritsar Junction (123 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pong Dam Lake Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pong Dam Lake Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pong Dam Lake Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Pong Dam Lake Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Pong Dam Lake Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Maharana Pratap Sagar, Nagrota Surian, Sakri, Kangra, Dhami State, Haripur Guler.
